title: Below are deep-research prompts designed to generate a litigation
content:
Below are deep-research prompts designed to generate a litigation-grade, bulletproof argument for Padavano v. Miami Dade College. Each prompt is written so it can be run independently in advanced legal research systems (Lexis/Westlaw analogs, AI research agents, expert reports, or court-facing memoranda). They are sequenced logically so outputs can be stitched into a single master brief, expert declaration, or summary-judgment opposition. No prompts rely on speculation; each is grounded in the documentary record you already possess, including the Complaint, Answer, ADA correspondence, IMC findings, and damages summary .

⸻

Prompt 1 – ADA Failure-to-Accommodate: Telework as a Reasonable Accommodation
Conduct a comprehensive legal analysis under the ADA and ADAAA addressing when full-time remote work constitutes a reasonable accommodation. Focus on Eleventh Circuit precedent and persuasive federal authority. Apply the law to a role whose essential functions are digital marketing management and adjunct instruction, both demonstrably remote-capable. Explicitly analyze why a staged, partial in-office schedule fails as a reasonable accommodation for chronic, non-episodic disabilities, referencing EEOC guidance on telework and the employer’s obligation to consider whether the job can be performed from home. Tie conclusions directly to the facts alleged in the Complaint and admitted in part by the Defendant’s Answer, including the IMC’s disability finding .
